content.problemStatement:
You are given a tree (a simple connected acyclic graph) with NNN vertices, as well as two integers KKK and DDD.

The distance between two vertices uuu and vvv in the tree, denoted dist(u,v)\text{dist}(u, v)dist(u,v), is defined to be the number of edges on the (unique) path between uuu and vvv in the tree.

You would like to color each vertex of the tree either red or blue.

Exactly KKK vertices must be colored red, while the remaining N−KN-KN−K vertices must be colored red.

The disparity of this coloring is defined as follows:


For each vertex uuu, define BuB_uBu​ to be the minimum possible value of dist(u,v)\text{dist}(u, v)dist(u,v) across all vertices vvv such that uuu and vvv have different colors.

In particular, if no valid vertices vvv with the opposite color exist, we define Bu=NB_u = NBu​=N.
Then, the disparity of this coloring is defined to be the maximum value in the array BBB, i.e. max⁡u(Bu)\max_u(B_u)maxu​(Bu​).
You are yet to decide on a coloring.

Count the number of ways to color the vertices of the tree such that:


Exactly KKK vertices are colored red; and
The disparity of the coloring is **at most** DDD.
Two colorings are considered different if there exists some vertex uuu that is colored red in one coloring and blue in the other.

The answer may be large, so output it modulo 998 244 353998\,244\,353998244353.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains three space-separated integers N,K,N, K,N,K, and DDD — the size of the tree, the requisite number of red vertices, and the maximum allowed disparity.
The next N−1N-1N−1 lines describe the edges of the tree. The iii-th of these N−1N-1N−1 lines contains two space-separated integers uiu_iui​ and viv_ivi​, denoting an edge between uiu_iui​ and viv_ivi​.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line a single integer: the number of valid colorings modulo 998 244 353998\,244\,353998244353.
constraints: The input edges describe a tree on {1,2,…,N}\{1, 2, \ldots, N\}{1,2,…,N}.
The sum of N4N^4N4 over all test cases won't exceed 1004100^41004.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: We have two vertices and K=1K = 1K=1, so the only possible colorings are one red vertex and one blue vertex.
Both colorings have a disparity of 111, so they're both valid. The answer is 222.
Test case 222: There are 666 possible colorings with two red and two blue vertices. All of them are valid.
Test case 333: Any coloring with three red vertices has a disparity of 222, which is not ≤D=1\le D = 1≤D=1. So, the answer is 000.
